title: Google Calendar MCP connector (egress-gated) + safe-title privacy
description: Add a Google Calendar connector that implements CalendarConnector via the external-MCP subprocess pattern, gated behind a new default-false fail-closed cloud-calendar egress axis and a `calendar` config section. Free/busy is read and events are written through a GoogleCalendarToolAdapter (tool discovery via listTools, calls via callTool) with a stub adapter for tests since real tools surface only after OAuth. Cloud events carry only finding.calendarSafeTitle; the connector refuses with a clear message when the axis is off or no token is present, and the unfit-for-unattended-OAuth caveat is documented with the .ics fallback recommendation.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) completes with zero errors after adding the calendar config section, the egress guard, and src/calendar/google-connector.ts.
`npm run typecheck` confirms the Google connector structurally satisfies the CalendarConnector interface with zero type errors.

A vitest asserts that with calendar.egress.cloudCalendar=false the Google write path refuses with an error message naming the calendar.egress.cloudCalendar flag and never constructs the MCP client (no listTools/callTool calls on the stub).

A vitest with the axis enabled and a stub GoogleCalendarToolAdapter asserts writeEvents maps each scheduled item to an event whose summary equals finding.calendarSafeTitle and whose serialized payload contains none of the finding's evidence strings or full title.
A vitest asserts readFreeBusy via the stub adapter returns BusyInterval[] that the Sprint 1 slotter consumes end-to-end to produce a ProposedPlan.

A vitest asserts that a simulated adapter error does not include token values or MCP env in the thrown message (sanitized like src/mcp/external-client.ts:69), and a source/doc scan confirms the connector or docs/calendar.md states hosted OAuth is unfit for unattended runs and recommends the .ics fallback.

nonGoals: Do not perform live OAuth or call real Google APIs in tests — use the stub GoogleCalendarToolAdapter.
Do not send finding.evidence, the full title, or tags to the cloud connector — only calendarSafeTitle.
Do not enable the cloud-calendar egress axis by default — it defaults false and fails closed.
Do not write events when the axis is off or no token is present.
Do not log or embed token values or MCP env in any error message.
stopConditions: The axis-off refusal test, the safe-title mapping test, the free/busy-into-slotter test, and the no-secret-leak test pass under `npm test`, and `npm run build` is green.
The Google connector implements the same CalendarConnector interface that the .ics connector implements.
definitionOfDone: With calendar.egress.cloudCalendar disabled (the default), any attempt to write to Google refuses with a message naming the flag and never opens an MCP client. With the axis enabled and a token present, the connector reads free/busy and writes events through the MCP adapter, sending only calendarSafeTitle so no sensitive 'why' leaves the device, and tokens/MCP env never appear in errors. The connector and docs state that hosted OAuth is unfit for unattended/cron runs and point those runs to the .ics fallback. The Google connector and the .ics connector are interchangeable behind CalendarConnector.
assumptions: Cloud-calendar write is egress; gated by a default-false fail-closed axis modeled on EgressGuard (src/medical/egress.ts:5-58) and a `calendar` config section modeled on MedicalSectionSchema (src/config/schema.ts:374-401).

Google MCP tool names surface only post-OAuth (research §3a line 135) — connector written against a GoogleCalendarToolAdapter interface with a stub for tests; live discovery via listTools/callTool mirrors src/mcp/external-client.ts:81-103.
Only calendarSafeTitle leaves the device (research §3a line 135).
0600 local-token sidecar path modeled on src/medical/whoop/whoop-token.ts for the interactive Google path; secret sanitization modeled on src/mcp/external-client.ts:69.
Hosted OAuth is unfit for unattended/cron (research §3a line 135) — scheduled runs use the .ics fallback from Sprint 2.
outOfScope: Implementing the actual OAuth authorize handshake / token acquisition (provisioned out-of-band into the 0600 sidecar, as with WHOOP).
Two-way sync or reading existing event details beyond free/busy.
The approval gate (Sprint 4) — this sprint provides the connector the gate will call.
generatorNotes: Add a `calendar` section to BoberConfigSchema in src/config/schema.ts mirroring MedicalSectionSchema (src/config/schema.ts:374-401): `{ egress: { cloudCalendar: boolean default false }, connector: 'ics'|'google' default 'ics', timezone?: string }`. Create a small guard (e.g. src/calendar/calendar-egress.ts) modeled on EgressGuard (src/medical/egress.ts) with assertCloudCalendarAllowed() that throws naming calendar.egress.cloudCalendar when off — and check it BEFORE constructing any MCP client (mirror runWhoopSync axis-off branch in src/cli/commands/medical.ts:53-62). Create src/calendar/google-connector.ts implementing CalendarConnector via a GoogleCalendarToolAdapter interface { listTools(); callTool(name, args) } so tests inject a stub; the production adapter wraps ExternalMcpServer (src/mcp/external-client.ts). writeEvents maps each PlanItem to an event payload with summary = finding.calendarSafeTitle (fallback to a generic placeholder like 'Focus block' when absent) and NO evidence/title/tags. Sanitize errors per src/mcp/external-client.ts:69 (strip KEY=VALUE, never include mcpEnv/token). Add a 0600 token sidecar reader modeled on src/medical/whoop/whoop-token.ts; refuse with a clear message + .ics suggestion when the token is absent. Document the unattended-OAuth caveat in a module doc-comment and/or docs/calendar.md. Honor ESM .js imports, Zod, node:fs/promises, type-only imports.
evaluatorNotes: Verify axis-off path throws naming calendar.egress.cloudCalendar and the stub adapter records zero listTools/callTool calls. Verify writeEvents summary equals calendarSafeTitle and the serialized payload excludes evidence/full-title. Verify free/busy from the stub flows into the Sprint 1 slotter. Verify error sanitization (inject a fake token into an error path and assert it is redacted). Confirm the caveat text exists. Confirm both connectors satisfy CalendarConnector (typecheck).
